content:
Welcome to the final discussion for June 2017 content regarding Commodities and Futures. A reminder to
review the disclaimer: I am not a licensed commodity trade advisor. Everything discussed should be viewed
as paper trade only.
This is the ICT Mentorship, June 2017, ICT Index Trading Concepts, Lesson Five: Index Trade Setups.

section_title: AMPLIFYING RANGE PROJECTIONS FOR INDEX TRADE SETUPS
content:
Building on Lesson Four's range projections and objectives, we will amplify these for index trade setups.
The approach is simplistic, which is exactly what you should be looking for in setups. Over-complicating
leads to a million questions and prevents understanding.

- AM TREND TO DISCOUNT ARRAY
The first setup is the 'Two Session Up Close'. When looking for the AM Trend to return down to a discount
array, we are actually looking for:
- An Index SMT Divergence on the lows.
- Comparing the lows from the London session into the 9:30 AM New York equities open.
- The AM session is usually going to reach an expansion move, which can be a higher timeframe PDA, but
generally, you want to be holding out for time of day, 10:30 to 11:00 AM, typically understood in Forex as
the London close.
- We want to be holding on to it with the idea that it's going to expand going into the lunch hour.

- LUNCH HOUR AND PM SESSION ANALYSIS
When we get into the lunch hour, it can go into consolidation. Again, we can be looking for:
- A retracement back down below the lows formed in lunch.
- Or returning back to a fair value Gap or bullish order block seen inside of the lunch hour.
- At the lows that form in the lunch hour, and the low that forms immediately after 1:00 PM New York time,
those lows are going to be compared against the two other averages (Dow and NASDAQ when looking at
the S&P) for Index SMT Divergence at these specific price points. There's no ambiguity here; it's highly
specific.
- We're going to be looking for the indices to diverge. One of them, not necessarily the E-mini S&P, if the
NASDAQ fails to go lower, that in itself supports the idea that the S&P should rally and go higher.

- PM SESSION TIME OF DAY AND PROFIT TAKING
As we go into the later portions of the day, going into the close, we're going to be relying on time of day,
just as we did with the AM session. When I say relying on time of day, we have to do what we can to hold
for time.
- Remember, the day is going to expand as we get closer to the closing price or closing hour.
- We want to get the maximum amount of daily range in our favor. So, if we're long in the AM or PM
session, it's most beneficial when we're holding for the maximum potential range that can fulfill in the daily
candle.
- If we're looking at the E-mini S&P and we see bullishness underway, and we're long, and it's only 2:00 in
the afternoon, and we've seen some 'handles' in our favor (paper profit), don't be aggressive about trying to
take those profits off the table.
- You want to allow the time of day to come to a higher level of delivery on price, which obviously will lend
well to you getting a higher profit. The more you hold towards the close, the more opportunity there is for
you to make more of a range.

- PD ARRAY OBJECTIVES AND PM SESSION STAGES
The AM session and the PM session highs initially are going to be most likely a 15-minute or a 60-minute
PD array. Since we're looking at a 'Two Session Up Close,' both the AM session and PM session PD array
are going to be a premium array.
- Again, it could be a 15-minute premium array or a 60-minute premium array, but you're aiming for that for
both sessions.
- On the diagram, the PM session going into the close could be two stages. It could fall short of a 15-minute
or 60-minute PDA and start to retrace, then look for an additional ramp up that last hour going into the
close until it reaches the 15-minute or 60-minute PD array (premium array).

- AM TREND TO PREMIUM ARRAY
Now, we're looking at the 'Two Sessions Down Close'. Again, in the morning, when we see price return to a
premium array:
- At that moment, we're going to be comparing the other two indices (NASDAQ and the Dow) and we're
going to be comparing the highs for Index SMT Divergence.
- This is from London into the 9:30 AM equities opening.
- We're going to be holding until 10:30 to 11:00 AM, relying on time of day to get the maximum amount of
range.
- As we get close to 11:00 AM, there's more likelihood of a consolidation or pause going into the lunch hour
at noon.

- LUNCH HOUR AND POST-LUNCH WEAKNESS
During the lunch hour, we're going to be looking at the highs comparable with the NASDAQ and the Dow
futures against the S&P.
- Again, this can be a return back to a fair value gap or a bearish order block, or closing of a liquidity void,
and then going lower.
- Or, it could run the lunch high for buy stops and then reject. But you're looking at the highs during the
lunch hour and you're looking at the high that forms post-New York lunch.
- When that happens, and it forms, there's always going to be some measure of short-term high that forms
immediately after lunch. If you're looking for a 'Two Session Down Close' profile to unfold, projecting that
daily range, we're going to be looking for weakness immediately after the lunch hour closes.
- So, from 1:00 PM in the afternoon New York time, we're immediately going to be hunting and stalking the
E-mini S&P's short-term high. If price was rallying up, we're going to be looking for reasons to suspect a
fair value play for return to fair value for selling short, and bearish Order Block, fair value gap, or liquidity
void, or a run above or a move above the New York lunch high.

- PM SESSION HOLDING STRATEGY AND PD ARRAYS
In the PM session, again, we have to rely on time of day. Don't just take a couple 'handles' of profit, hold on
to it.
- Head towards the close, at least try to hold on until 3:00 PM Bond close. And if you can do all you can to
hold on into 4:00 PM, it's ideal to do that.
- The AM session and the PM session intraday lows again are going to be many times lined up with a 15 to
60-minute PD array. Since we're looking at 'Two Session Down Close' projected range, it's going to be a 15
to 60-minute discount array.
- Now, in the PM session, again, that many times can happen as a two-stage short-term rally where early
profit will be taken. If it does not do that short-term bounce at a logical 15-minute or 60-minute discount
array, hold for it to break another leg lower going into the close. And then that later point at which it trades
lower, many times will trade into that 15-minute or 60-minute discount array.

- AM SESSION SMT DIVERGENCE AND HOLDING TIME
Alright, we're looking at the 'AM Rally PM Reversal' projected range. In the AM session, we're going to be
looking at the three averages (NASDAQ, Dow against the S&P), and we're going to be comparing the Index
SMT on the lows.
- So, we're going to be looking for a divergence. One of them will fail to go lower; that will signal the time to
be a buyer of the E-mini S&P for the New York session.
- We're going to be relying on time of day, holding until we get very close to 11:00 AM or just past 11:00 AM.
- As we get into the lunch hour, again, ahead of noon, many times we'll see a little bit of profit-taking
retracement.

- PM SESSION SELL SETUP AND DISCOUNT ARRAY
CONTEXT
Then in the afternoon, we're going to be looking for a rally to sell into. And we're going to hold that until we
get to time of day and price.
- So, we have to refer back to that discount array in the AM session that caused the initial rally.
- If it blends well with a higher timeframe discount array, obviously we expect a short-term bounce, maybe
back into consolidation or resuming higher to take out the daily high.
- But if the daily AM session discount array is not a higher timeframe discount array, we'll look for
continuational downside and take out the intraday low.

- PM HIGH PD ARRAY AND NESTED PREMIUM ARRAYS
In the afternoon, that high is going to be a 15 to 60-minute premium PD array. In the AM session, in this
case, it creates a lower high than that which is formed in the PM session.
- If that short-term high forms before lunch, if it's below a 15-minute or a 60-minute premium array, then
we're going to be anticipating before it even happens, the initial move outside of the London lunch around
1:00 PM in the afternoon New York time, then it will rally up to the 15-minute or 60-minute premium array
and then anticipate a higher timeframe Market reversal to fall as indicated in this diagram.
- Note: you're thinking 15-minute is not a higher timeframe, and neither is a 60-minute, but we're going to
be looking for an overlap or a nested premium array seen on the 15-minute or 60-minute and daily or four
- hour. So the higher timeframe confluence with nested ideas of Premium array would be our catalyst for this
PM session reversal.

- AM SESSION SMT DIVERGENCE AND HOLDING
STRATEGY
The 'AM Decline PM Reversal' setup. In the morning, we'll be looking for the Index SMT Divergence on the
highs, going from the London session into the 9:30 AM equities opening.
- One of those three indices will be the bearish divergence, indicating a New York AM session decline.
- We're going to be holding again for a very minimum 10:30 AM, and closer to 11:00 AM.
- At 11:00 AM, anticipate some measure of consolidation or retracement going into lunch hour.

- PM SESSION LOW TARGET AND PREMIUM ARRAY
CONTEXT
In the PM session, we anticipate a run on the daily low or the low seen in the lunch hour.
- Again, we hold on to the later portion of the day, at least try to aim for holding until 3:00 PM Bond close
New York time if at all possible.
- If it gets to the premium array that saw price decline from the AM session, we could see price resume and
go lower.
- Or, if that AM session premium array is not a higher timeframe premium array, we can anticipate further
upside and then the intraday high being taken out.

- PM SESSION LOW AND NESTED DISCOUNT ARRAY
CONFIRMATION
In this diagram, the AM session low is suggesting the low forms above a 15-minute or 60-minute PD array.
- Therefore, a lower low could be expected in the afternoon, trading down into these 15-minute or 60
- minute PD array. That would cause our reason to justify a nested discount array.
- So, if that discount array in the afternoon forms at a 15-minute or 60-minute discount array, and it is also a
nested higher timeframe (four-hour or daily) discount array, that further bolsters the idea that this is going to
be a market reversal in the afternoon, and we can trade it accordingly.

- AM SESSION DIVERGENCE AND
CONSOLIDATION
The 'Consolidation AM Decline PM Rally' setup. Again, initially when the market makes an attempt to rally
up into a premium array, we're going to be looking at the indices to diverge bearishly at the highs
comparably.
- We'll be holding for again, ideally around 10:30 to 11:00 AM in the morning New York time.
- Then anticipating after 11:00 AM, some measure of consolidation to retracement higher going into the
lunch hour.

- PM SESSION SMT AND RANGE
EXPECTATIONS
Then we will be comparing the SMT Divergence that should form at the lows comparably across the
NASDAQ, Dow, and E-mini futures.
- And looking for time of day to support the idea of an expansion on the upside.
- If it's consolidation, we don't expect it to go all the way into the close. In other words, it's going to be an
early short-term high that forms that will be later on a decline seen going into the close. So we're not going
to be holding for the rest of the day.
- We're going to be relying on time of day, so generally around 2:00 in the afternoon it creates that
comparable high in the PM session.

- PD ARRAYS AND INTRADAY LOW
CONSIDERATIONS
The AM session low is many times a 15-minute or 60-minute discount PD array. In the afternoon, again, is
also a 15 to 60-minute PD array or discount array.
- The afternoon low could run just the lunch low or it can run the intraday low formed in the AM session.
Again, as a reminder, how you determine which one it's going to be is if the AM session bounces into the
lunch off of a higher timeframe nested level that's also seen on a 15-minute or a 60-minute discount array,
then we don't expect the intraday low to be taken out in the afternoon session.
- But if that AM session low is forming above a higher timeframe discount array, then we would anticipate a
lower run on the intraday low in the PM session, and then the rally or increase in price after that stop run
has been seen.

- AFTERNOON HIGH TARGET
The afternoon high is going to be seen with a 15-minute or 60-minute PD array, and we would anticipate
not further upside, but a retracement and gravitation back to the equilibrium price point of the day, basically
back to the middle.

- AM SESSION DIVERGENCE AND
CONSOLIDATION
The 'Consolidation AM Rally PM Decline' setup. In the morning, we're going to be comparing the Index
SMT Divergence at the lows comparably against the Dow and NASDAQ with the S&P E-mini.
- And we're going to be holding for a minimum of 10:30 to 11:00 AM in the morning New York time.
- At 11:00 AM, we would anticipate some measure of consolidation and or retracement down into the lunch
hour.

- PM SESSION RALLY AND LOW TARGETS
After 1:00 PM is seen in New York, we would anticipate some measure of a rally again, and we would be
comparing SMT Divergence at the highs comparably across the three averages.
- And we will be holding for again, around 2:00 PM minimum, looking for a run into the short-term lows.

- PD ARRAYS AND HIGHER TIMEFRAME
CONFLUENCE
The AM session high will be a 15-minute or 60-minute PD array or premium array. The afternoon will be a
15-minute or 60-minute PD array. Now, the afternoon can run the lunch hour high for buy stops.
- If the AM session high forms at a higher timeframe (four-hour or daily) premium array, then we anticipate
a reversal.
- If the AM session does not form the short-term high going into the lunch at a higher timeframe (four-hour
or daily) premium array, we would anticipate the rally after the New York lunch to run the intraday high up
into a four-hour or daily PD array or premium array and then decline after that.
- So, it's a very simple measure of what's causing the high to form in the AM session. If it falls short of a
premium array that's seen on a four-hour or daily, chances are it's probably going to rally again harder to
reach for that level and then make a reversal.

- AFTERNOON LOW TARGET
The afternoon low, again reaching into the 2:00 PM hour, as many times is going to be a 15-minute to 60
- minute discount array.
EXAMPLE:

Just as an example, to show you what it looks like, again, I'll give you an example of a 'Consolidation AM
Rally PM Decline' that's what was seen today at the time of this recording. I'll show you here as an example:
- Look at the furthest down chart on the bottom, it's the Dow. We can see going into the AM session of
June 2017 on the 23rd (Friday), we can see that the low failed to go lower into the AM session.
- For the NASDAQ (middle chart), we can see very easily and obviously that the NASDAQ was able to go
lower, and the S&P was able to go lower. So the Dow was able to buck that trend, indicating there were
strong buyers stepping in, and the S&P rallied as a result.

section_title: EXAMPLE (CONT.): PM SESSION DIVERGENCES AND S&P ACTION
content:
Then we went into the lunch hour, and then immediately after the lunch hour (12:00 to 1:00 PM in New
York time), that begins our PM trend or afternoon session for the equities or indices.
- Again, at the bottom, we can see that the Dow, from the high formed in lunch, made a lower high, was
unable to rally above it in the PM session and went lower.
- In the NASDAQ (middle chart), we can see that the NASDAQ made a higher high than that which was
seen in the lunch hour.
- And the S&P was unable to make a higher high that was seen in the lunch hour and price trades down as
a result, and back into the equilibrium or middle of the range.

section_title: EXAMPLE (CONT.): S&P AM RALLY AND PD ARRAY CONFLUENCE
content:
Notice in the S&P that during the AM session that rally traded all the way up into an old premium array (that
old high back here). So, blending these ideas, you can see how using the 15 to 60-minute PD array as
objectives and targets, and also to frame the context of what makes these intraday swings occur when
looking at the S&P.

When we get into the August content, I give you the top-down analysis PDF. It gives you a flowchart
perspective, and I'll walk you through the scenarios that lead to these specific projected ranges and how to
trade them accordingly in a trading plan format.
- Until I talk to you again next week, we talk about stock trading, doing short-term trading and swing trading
with stocks on a month by month basis.
- That will complete the delivery of June 2017's content. In the next month, in July, we'll be looking at 'Mega
trades' across all the asset classes as well. Until then, I wish you good luck and good trading.
